profile.name: John
profile.headline: Strong forehand base, improving backhand consistency
profile.summary: John creates solid forehand pressure and is trending in the right direction, but still leaks points through backhand errors and passive return decisions.

milestones.title: Backhand error rate below 40%
milestones.body: Down from 46% to 43% over the last 4 matches.

milestones.title: Forehand remains primary point-builder
milestones.body: Forehand still leads winner contribution and rally control.

recommended_drills.title: Serve + first ball forehand pattern
recommended_drills.description: Use strength to own the second shot.

blocks.title: Technical warm-up
blocks.description: Backhand rhythm, spacing, and controlled tempo.

blocks.title: Pressure repeaters
blocks.description: Backhand under movement with target-zone scoring.

blocks.title: Pattern play
blocks.description: Build points into forehand finish after safe backhand neutralisation.

blocks.title: Competitive games
blocks.description: Score-based points starting from return situations.

strengths: Forehand remains the cleanest attacking platform.
Winner production is trending up match to match.
weaknesses: Backhand error burden is still the main leak.
Returns can become too aggressive too early in the rally.
coach_weaknesses: Backhand shape breaks down under pace.
coach_recommendations: Use forehand patterns after safe neutral balls.
tactical: Keep points cross-court longer before redirecting.
Do not force line changes from unstable backhand positions.

auto_recommendations.title: Primary focus
auto_recommendations.body: Prioritise backhand under pressure because it currently creates the biggest drag on performance.

auto_recommendations.title: Strength to build from
auto_recommendations.body: Anchor sessions around forehand drive to protect confidence while improving weaker areas.

auto_recommendations.title: Benchmark gap
auto_recommendations.body: Largest benchmark gap currently sits in overhead error %, so training should link directly to that measure.

profile.name: Jenny
profile.headline: Net game improving, needs better timing and patience
profile.summary: Jenny is aggressive at the net but often moves forward too early, leading to avoidable errors and unstable first-volley decisions.

milestones.title: First-volley stability improving
milestones.body: Cleaner net entry decisions across the last 3 matches.

milestones.title: Forehand transition patterns more controlled
milestones.body: Rushed forehand attacks are reducing in neutral phases.

recommended_drills.title: Split-step to first volley block
recommended_drills.description: Improve timing before closing the net.

recommended_drills.title: Forehand approach patterning
recommended_drills.description: Build safer attack triggers before moving forward.

recommended_drills.title: Return patience games
recommended_drills.description: Delay acceleration until the second or third neutral ball.

blocks.title: Movement preparation
blocks.description: Split-step timing and forward balance into first volley position.

blocks.title: Controlled first volley
blocks.description: Land softer, safer first volleys into margin zones.

blocks.title: Approach decision drills
blocks.description: Train clearer triggers before committing forward.

blocks.title: Pressure games
blocks.description: Score only when net entries start from the right ball.

strengths: Aggressive net intent creates pressure when timing is right.
Forehand still gives Jenny the cleanest route into attack.
weaknesses: Early forward movement creates avoidable volley errors.
Backhand consistency still dips under pace.
coach_strengths: Positive attacking mindset at the front of the court.
Willing to take space early when confident.
coach_weaknesses: Can overcommit before the rally is ready for a net move.
Backhand shape gets rushed when defending fast exchanges.
coach_recommendations: Train net entry timing before increasing volley aggression.
Keep the first volley safer and deeper.
tactical: Build one more neutral ball before moving through the middle.
Use forehand approach balls to control tempo before closing.

auto_recommendations.title: Primary focus
auto_recommendations.body: Improve first-volley timing so aggressive court positioning becomes more efficient.

auto_recommendations.title: Strength to build from
auto_recommendations.body: Use forehand transition quality as the main route into attack.

auto_recommendations.title: Benchmark gap
auto_recommendations.body: Biggest cost currently comes from total error % and unstable volley execution.

profile.name: Heidi
profile.headline: Defensive stability improving, overhead still a weakness
profile.summary: Heidi defends well and absorbs pace effectively, but still loses too many points under lob pressure and needs more confidence finishing overhead opportunities.

milestones.title: Overhead confidence under pressure
milestones.body: Decision quality has improved, but contact errors are still too high.

milestones.title: Defensive absorption more stable
milestones.body: Heidi is sustaining longer rallies before giving away neutral balls.

recommended_drills.title: Overhead recovery footwork
recommended_drills.description: Improve balance and spacing after deep lobs.

recommended_drills.title: Backhand absorb-and-reset
recommended_drills.description: Hold shape better under pace before redirecting.

recommended_drills.title: Defend-to-attack transition games
recommended_drills.description: Convert defensive stability into cleaner finishing opportunities.

blocks.title: Footwork warm-up
blocks.description: Drop-step, crossover, and recovery movement for lob coverage.

blocks.title: Contact ladder
blocks.description: Build repeatable overhead timing from simple feeds into pressure feeds.

blocks.title: Defensive reset patterns
blocks.description: Absorb pace and recover structure before finishing the point.

blocks.title: Competitive overhead games
blocks.description: Reward clean overhead decisions rather than forced finishes.

strengths: Defensive rally tolerance is improving steadily.
Forehand still gives the clearest opportunity to neutralise and rebuild.
weaknesses: Overhead error rate remains the biggest performance leak.
Backhand can lose penetration when defending deep.
coach_strengths: Good willingness to stay in the point and absorb pace.
Improved shape in longer defensive exchanges.
coach_weaknesses: Overhead contact still breaks down under lob pressure.
Can become passive after defending two or three balls well.
coach_recommendations: Train overhead spacing before increasing finish aggression.
Build one positive transition ball after deep defence.
tactical: Use deeper central resets before looking for the next attack.
Do not rush the overhead finish from unstable footwork.

auto_recommendations.title: Primary focus
auto_recommendations.body: Reduce overhead errors first, because that is the biggest current drag on point conversion.

auto_recommendations.title: Strength to build from
auto_recommendations.body: Keep using defensive tolerance as the base while adding one controlled finishing pattern.

auto_recommendations.title: Benchmark gap
auto_recommendations.body: The largest gap sits in overhead error %, so that should anchor the next training block.
